channel.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
channel.description:
<!-- Please, follow this template to be as efficient as possible. Before opening a new feature, please check that it hasn't been already added. -->
# Description <!-- Try to give a detailed explanation of what do you expect of this feature --> Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`.
# Examples <!-- Do you know examples that could inspire the community? Let us know! --> When we do `actions.router.set('#some-div')` the site should scroll to the div with that id. # Possible solution <!-- If you have already thought of a possible solution, let us know! --> ![fd-hash-links|308x500](upload://wRRmgInrtRfJyhr2mt4jgNTuxOF.png)

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Maybe we(the community) should get together and create a fork

Hey Nikolay, that’s awesome!

If you (or a group of people) are willing to take over the development and maintenance of the project, we can give you access to whatever you need (GitHub, Community, Docs…). Do you want to set up a call to talk about it?

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: ni.bonev
item.description:
Yeah seems like the project has been completely abandoned. Maybe we(the community) should get together and create a fork before Automatic completely tries to kill Frontity. I think there are quite a few people who are still here and quite passionate about pushing this project forward.

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: jeff
item.description:
Hi guys, is there an update on this or has the development now stopped since the Automattic acquisition?

Thanks

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
Ooh okay, I get it know. Thanks for explaining it guys

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
cristianbote:

But I think this is about the internal links to arrive to the same page, load the data, and then ‘move’ the focus to the hashed value

Yep, I think that’s exactly the case we were trying to fix here.

  • Hash in SSR links work.
  • Hash in CSR links for the same page work.
  • Hash in CSR links for a different page don’t work.

I’ve made a video because I think it’s not trivial to understand: https://www.loom.com/share/4dd6a62c00334e21b96632c9941fc7ec

EDIT: Also relative links don’t work. That is probably more serious because it is more common. If you do:

actions.router.set("#why-gutenberg-and-frontity")

You will end up on https://frontity.org/#why-gutenberg-and-frontity, not on https://frontity.org/blog/connecting-gutenberg-and-frontity/#why-gutenberg-and-frontity.

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: cristianbote
item.description:
Oh I see. I was asking just because the #hash part of any link does not hit the server so expecting SSR based on the hash part should not be expected.

Specifically speaking about this I’ve tried it in incognito and it works for me – navigates to the hash section. But I think this is about the internal links to arrive to the same page, load the data, and then ‘move’ the focus to the hashed value.

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
Btw, what I meant is that if I go directly (SSR) to a link with a hash like this one:

https://frontity.org/blog/connecting-gutenberg-and-frontity/#how-did-we-implement-it

I expect to arrive directly to the How did we implement it? section of the post but it loads at the top of the page.

If after loading the post I click on the link #how-did-we-implement-it (CSR), that is included in the index, it scrolls until that section.

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
In case it affects somehow: I was taking a look at our blog, where we are using the twentytwenty-theme with the Link component, and the hash links seem to work in CSR but they don’t in SSR. You can see an example at our post about Gutenberg and Frontity.

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
Yes, I think it makes sense. Actually I was thinking if we should consider this a feature or a bug

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
@santosguillamot, maybe it makes to add this one to the roadmap for this Q?

Read full topic

item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: SantosGuillamot
item.description:
luisherranz:

@SantosGuillamot I guess we can move this back to “awaiting development”, right?

Yes, it makes sense. I have just changed it.

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
orballo:

Do you want me to open a PR draft with that code in case somebody else wants to pick it up from there in case I cannot continue with it?

Oh, yes please Open a PR and link it here.

@SantosGuillamot I guess we can move this back to “awaiting development”, right?

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
@luisherranz I have this on pause currently as the client that needed these changes told me they might not be necessary anymore, so I’m on hold.

If I remember right, I did some progress and finished the part for state.source.get() and actions.source.fetch(). Do you want me to open a PR draft with that code in case somebody else wants to pick it up from there in case I cannot continue with it?

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`

item.title: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
Description

Add support for hash links in @frontity/tiny-router.

Examples

When we do actions.router.set('#some-div') the site should scroll to the div with that id.

Possible solution

Read full topic
item.source: Add support for hash links in `@frontity/tiny-router`
